title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs service outcomes, boundaries, and server lifecycle from bounded request and server evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.
Distinguish process, listener, connection, stream, request, handler, goroutine, dependency, and durable-state lifetimes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Service Outcome Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Service Outcome Contract Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Service Outcome Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Service Outcome Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Service Outcome Contract, keep Service Outcome Contract connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 3265 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Service Outcome Contract has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.

For Go server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Service Outcome Contract, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: More routes and higher request throughput necessarily mean the service creates more user value.

options.text: Declare Go server method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the review Service Outcome Contract case
Predict Go server resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the review Service Outcome Contract case
Run the smallest deterministic Go server request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the review Service Outcome Contract case
Inject one changed Go server failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the review Service Outcome Contract case
